paradigm: CMT v3-default (MLP-only + residual-passthrough) 5-bb in_vivo retest verdict

mistral_7b_v03.v1_comparison: axis 99 v1: rel_std=0.0120 mean=0.0163 — v3 rel_std HIGHER (signal_preserved_or_amplified, NOT collapsed)

gemma_2_9b.v1_comparison: axis 99 v1 baseline: rel=1.000 SATURATED at every layer. v3: rel_std=0.0292 (saturation GONE)

mistral_nemo_12b.v1_comparison: axis 99 v1 baseline: rel=1.000 SATURATED 100%. v3: rel_std=0.0225 saturation_frac=0.0 (informative)

mistral_nemo_12b_v2_comparison.note: v2 mode (MLP-only + zero hook) gives WIDER rel distribution (rel_std=0.100 vs v3=0.023) on same backbone — v2 ALSO informative at 12B+, both modes recover signal vs v1's saturated rel=1.0

HCSF-A_SCALE_NORM.statement: magnitude renormalization (dY_norm = abs(dY)/sqrt(h_dim)) alone restores informative cross-scale comparability across 5 bb
HCSF-A_SCALE_NORM.decision_threshold: CONFIRMED if dY_norm std/mean ratio across 5 bb falls within [0.5, 2.0] band; FALSIFIED if any bb falls outside
HCSF-A_SCALE_NORM.note: mistral family (both 7B and 12B) consistently has ratio < 0.5 — high norm_dy mean with relatively narrow std. dY_norm metric is informative but NOT family-invariant. Mistral has tighter cross-layer norm_dy distribution than gemma.

HCSF-B_HID_DIM_BOUND.statement: MLP-only sub-module ablation (vs whole-layer) is required for rel<1 informative at 12B+
HCSF-B_HID_DIM_BOUND.decision_threshold: CONFIRMED if Mistral-Nemo-12B v3 rel std > 0.05 AND v1 rel std < 0.005 (saturation gone); FALSIFIED if v3 rel std remains < 0.005
HCSF-B_HID_DIM_BOUND.note: v3 rel_std=0.0225 is > 0.005 threshold (saturation GONE), but BELOW the pre-registered > 0.05 cut-off. v3 mode IS informative on 12B (rel<<1, saturation_frac=0) — qualitative HCSF-B confirmed, quantitative HCSF-B threshold revision needed (post-hoc → re-pre-register required per raw#10). v2-mode comparison run gives rel_std=0.100 on same backbone — v2 satisfies the pre-registered > 0.05 threshold.

HCSF-C_ARTIFACT_OF_HYBRID.statement: gemma-2-9b hybrid attention has separate degeneracy cause beyond MLP saturation
HCSF-C_ARTIFACT_OF_HYBRID.decision_threshold: CONFIRMED if gemma-2-9b v3 rel std remains < 0.01 even after MLP-only fix AND gemma-2-2b v3 rel std > 0.05; REJECTED if gemma-2-9b v3 rel std > 0.05
HCSF-C_ARTIFACT_OF_HYBRID.note: gemma-2-9b v3 rel_std=0.0292 is > 0.01 threshold. Hybrid attention is NOT a separate degeneracy cause — MLP-only + passthrough fix recovers informative regime on hybrid attention models too. HCSF-C falsified: v1 saturation was an artifact of whole-layer ablation, not of hybrid-attention architecture per se.

implication_mk_xii_phase_3b_70b.current_status: 1/3 hypotheses CONFIRMED (HCSF-B partial), 0/3 fully CONFIRMED, 1/3 REJECTED (HCSF-C), 1/3 FALSIFIED (HCSF-A)
implication_mk_xii_phase_3b_70b.70b_justification_path: WEAKENED — pre-registered hypothesis confirmation pattern below 2/3 floor. Phase 3b 70B dispatch should NOT proceed on HCSF-A/C alone.
actionable_next_steps: Re-pre-register HCSF-A with revised threshold informed by mistral-family tighter ratio (could be e.g. [0.3, 2.5])
HCSF-C closure: gemma family-internal scaling (2b/9b/27b) confirms hybrid-attn is NOT the obstruction — task #11 (gemma 2b/27b) UNBLOCKED to proceed
70B dispatch should wait for either HCSF-A revision PASS or new hypothesis battery covering scale-invariance
v2-mode comparison shows v2 rel_std (0.100) > v3 rel_std (0.023) on same backbone — v2 may be preferred mode for distribution-spread testing while v3 is preferred for residual-stream cleanliness

llama_3_1_8b_deferred.note: Llama-3.1-8B requires HF gated-repo access; user dancinlife not currently authorized. Re-dispatch upon HF access grant. NON-blocking for current verdict (4/5 ungated bb measured).
llama_3_1_8b_deferred.v1_comparison_baseline: axis 99 Phase 3a Llama-3.1-8B was measured (HF token had access at that time): rel_min=0.01, rel_max=0.0272, rel_std=0.005689, informative=True

gemma_2_9b_critical_for_task_11.task_description: gemma family-internal 2b/27b dispatch for scale-decoupling closure
gemma_2_9b_critical_for_task_11.rationale: gemma-2-9b v3 PASS (rel_std=0.029, saturation_frac=0.0, informative=True). HCSF-C REJECTED — hybrid attention is NOT a separate degeneracy cause. Therefore gemma-2-2b (informative, this run) and gemma-2-27b (HF gated, may need access) can be measured directly with v3 mode. Task #11 dispatch may proceed.

raw_10_caveats: HCSF-A FALSIFIED: 2/4 bb out of [0.5, 2.0] band — pre-registered threshold may be too narrow. Threshold revision = re-pre-register required (NOT done in this run). Mistral family consistently ratio<0.5; gemma family consistently ratio>0.7.

HCSF-B PARTIAL: v3 rel_std=0.023 < 0.05 threshold but >> 0.005 (saturation GONE). v2-mode comparison rel_std=0.100 satisfies > 0.05. The pre-registered threshold was tuned for v2-style 'wider distribution' expectation; v3 (passthrough) gives narrower informative distribution. Threshold revision = re-pre-register required.
HCSF-C cleanly REJECTED: gemma-2-9b v3 rel_std=0.029 >> 0.01. Hybrid attention is not the obstruction. This is the strongest finding of this run.
Llama-3.1-8B DEFERRED (HF gated). 4/5 bb measurement only — pre-register required all 5 bb for full closure. Verdict is conditional pending Llama re-dispatch.
v1 baselines for gemma-2-2b and Llama-3.1-8B not measured in axis 99 — cross-mode ablation comparison absent for those bb. v3 results stand alone for these two.

Pod 1 (phase 1) FAILED on 9B/12B due to 50GB container disk fill (HF cache bloat). Phase-2 pod with 100GB disk + HF_HOME=/workspace/hfcache succeeded. Disk-full failure was NOT a scientific failure but an infra config error. Re-pre-register text frozen; the pre-register did not specify infra disk size, so this is an addendum, not a hypothesis change.

HCSF-A out-of-band ratios (mistral 7B/12B = 0.372/0.393) reflect mistral family having tighter cross-layer norm_dy distribution than gemma — this is itself a finding (family-specific saturation profile) and may motivate a separate hypothesis test rather than HCSF-A revision.
